Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and potassium, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management of renal dysfunction.
